article_meta.title: Facebook X Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and CNN’s Clarissa Ward on the state of the war | The Conversation 02

reasoning: The article shows subtle Lean Left signals in the author’s own prose (e.g., describing Paxton as a "scandal-ridden MAGA darling" and characterizing Trump’s approval as "stubbornly low"), yet the model labeled it Center with very high confidence and offered generic, non-specific indicators. While the direction is only one class off, the 0.9 confidence is severely miscalibrated for a mixed/leaning piece, and the reasoning lacks specific citations to the author’s phrasing.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: Predicted Center, whereas author prose indicates Lean Left; within one class.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.9 is far too high given mixed but noticeable left-leaning signals; should be ~0.6-0.75.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Indicators were vague and structural, with no specific author phrases cited; missed clear loaded authorial language.
